title: Legal-source authority hierarchy, corpus scope, coverage matrix, and internal legal-source manifest contract
objective: PROPOSED — NOT CONTRACTED (owner approval required to move backlog -> ready). Establish the legal-source authority hierarchy (7 tiers: tier 1 current enacted/adopted law; tier 2 official amendments + project-specific legal instruments; tier 3 official agency interpretations; tier 4 official factual datasets; tier 5 third-party references cross-check only; tier 6 architect/client documents; tier 7 AI output = no authority), the legal-corpus coverage matrix with a per-domain status vocabulary (implemented | partial | missing | conflicting | unsupported | professional_review_required | not_applicable), official-source-registry channel additions for the DOB Construction Code, Local Law, DOB Rule, and Buildings Bulletin channels, a derived architect-benchmark discrepancy analysis (records ONLY the SHA-256 9442b5002e10b8ac0d9f78500db7cd4e8b34240e9155d0c61bbb51e00407ea85 + the observed sheet statements + the required-system-response discrepancies; does NOT commit the client PDF and does NOT infer project address/BBL/job number), and a versioned internal legal-source manifest schema (not promoted to a canonical cross-tier contract unless a cross-tier consumer justifies it). There must be NO single complete/compliant/buildable label while any material domain is unresolved. AI retrieves/classifies/drafts/explains only; humans approve legal interpretation. Full spec: project-control/reports/M3-CORPUS-REPLAN-PROPOSAL.md.
business_reason: M3 legal corpus is an undelivered dependency for M4 rule publication and every yard/coverage slice; the coverage matrix + authority hierarchy make completeness auditable and provenance-tiered instead of asserted from one manually-read chapter. This is also the corrective for the 'confirmed absence' overreach: a modifier is not 'confirmed absent' unless the declared search universe and cross-reference closure are complete.
inputs: M1-T001 accepted official-source registry research (docs/research/source-registry-drafts/*.json, docs/research/*.md) — provenance and access-governance baseline
docs/SOURCE_ACCESS_REGISTRY.md section 8 (Zoning Resolution portal, researched M1-T004; ingestion planned M3) — READ; extend with the four new legal channels

Architect benchmark: one-page sheet labeled '2 OF 2', SHA-256 9442b5002e10b8ac0d9f78500db7cd4e8b34240e9155d0c61bbb51e00407ea85; project/address/BBL/job/checked-by BLANK; observed statements R5 / 40x125=5,000 sf / §23-361 60% 3,000 allowed 2,850 proposed / §23-21 1.50 7,500 allowed 7,602 proposed / §23-422 35ft,45ft with 15ft setback 35 proposed / front 10 / rear 20 / side 5 — treat as ADVERSARIAL, not an answer key (B-010)
ZR §11-25 (district suffix inheritance), §12-10 (definitions incl. lot taxonomy), §23-361 / §23-21 / §23-422 (benchmark sections) — official ZR portal text
docs/PRODUCT_FLOW_AND_AI_BOUNDARIES.md (AI boundaries) and PRD source-tiering sections
allowed_paths: docs/LEGAL_CORPUS_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md (NEW — every declared domain with a status from the vocabulary; no aggregate compliant/buildable label)
docs/SOURCE_ACCESS_REGISTRY.md (ADDITIVE new sections only: DOB Construction Code, Local Law, DOB Rule, Buildings Bulletin channels; do not rewrite existing accepted rows)
packages/contracts/schemas/**/legal_source_manifest*.schema.json (NEW versioned internal manifest schema; additive; NOT promoted to canonical cross-tier contract without justification)
project-control/reports/M3-T001-architect-benchmark-analysis.md (hash + observations + required-response discrepancies ONLY; no PDF, no inferred identity)
forbidden_paths: Committing the client architect PDF anywhere in the repo (explicit owner authorization required)
Inferring or recording the benchmark project address, BBL, job number, or checked-by from metadata/filename
Any services/api/app runtime code, any connector implementation (this is authority/scope/contract only)
apps/web/** and any 3D/UI; any rule publication or Verified labeling; project-control/** except own reports; .claude/** except own agent-memory
acceptance_scenarios: AS-1 authority hierarchy: SOURCE_AUTHORITY_POLICY.md defines all 7 tiers with examples and the explicit rule that a project-specific agency determination is never generalized to another property without an approved legal interpretation; AI is tier 7 (no authority).

AS-2 coverage-matrix domain completeness: every domain in the replan proposal (zoning maps/district facts; ZR text; amendments/effective dates; definitions/interpretation; special districts/overlays; environmental & restrictive declarations; zoning-lot identity & recorded agreements; CPC/BSA/DOB approvals; Construction Code; DOB Rules/Bulletins/Local Laws; landmarks/historic; waterfront/flood/environmental; occupancy/use/building type; parking/loading; accessibility/fire-egress/structural) appears with exactly one status from the vocabulary.

AS-3 no false aggregate: the matrix emits NO single complete/compliant/buildable label; a machine check asserts the absence of such an aggregate field while any domain is missing/partial/unsupported/professional_review_required/conflicting.

AS-4 registry additions: SOURCE_ACCESS_REGISTRY.md gains four additive channel rows (Construction Code, Local Law, DOB Rule, Buildings Bulletin) each with official source identity, access mode, auth, published limits (or 'none published'), and a 'to verify at G1' marker; existing accepted rows are byte-unchanged.

AS-5 benchmark analysis honesty: the benchmark report records the SHA-256 and observed statements, surfaces 7,602 vs the sheet's stated 7,500 cap (102 sf over), records that no floor-area-exclusion schedule is supplied, that 60% lot coverage is not selectable until legal lot type + residence type are known, that the 15-ft setback branch depends on narrow-street/vertical-envelope conditions, that side-yard requirements depend on building type and may require two yards, and that project identity + zoning-lot documentation are missing — emitting NO buildable/compliant conclusion and NO inferred address/BBL.

AS-6 manifest schema versioned + validated: the legal_source_manifest schema is versioned, jsonschema-valid, and a sample manifest validates; it is not silently promoted to a canonical cross-tier contract.

NC-1 absence discipline: the deliverables never state a modifier is 'confirmed absent'; absence is expressed only as unsupported/not_evaluated/professional_review_required until closure (M3-T003) is complete.
NC-2 no PDF / no identity leak: a grep confirms the client PDF bytes are not committed and no project address/BBL/job number string appears in any output.
NC-3 tier discipline: no third-party reference (UpCodes etc.) is recorded as controlling provenance; it is tier 5 cross-check only.
risks: PROPOSAL ONLY: nothing here authorizes producer dispatch. Owner must approve the packet + dependency order (control-only PR) before backlog -> ready -> claim.

LEGAL BOUNDARY: authority-tier and matrix status assignments are classifications, not legal conclusions; any domain needing legal interpretation is professional_review_required, never resolved by the agent.
IDENTITY/PRIVACY: the client benchmark PDF must not be committed and its blank identity fields must not be inferred; B-010 tracks the sheet's absence from the repo.
NO NEW RUNTIME DEPENDENCY: authority/matrix/registry/manifest work is stdlib + existing jsonschema; no requests/httpx/scraping/AI framework.
requirement_refs: docs/GATES_AND_CHECKPOINTS.md (G0-G5 gate definitions; G6 reserved for rule publication)
docs/PRODUCT_FLOW_AND_AI_BOUNDARIES.md (AI retrieves/classifies/drafts/explains; humans approve legal interpretation)
harness_assignments: (defines, does not execute) — this task establishes the coverage vocabulary + authority tiers that the downstream source-fidelity, cross-reference-closure, lot/applicability, rule-behavior, architect-benchmark, and consumer-boundary harnesses assert against.
